data.title: Simmons First National Non-GAAP EPS of $0.54 beats by $0.06, revenue of $249M beats by $10.38M

data.title: Community Health Systems to sell Huntsville hospital for $450M
data.description:
• Community Health Systems (CYH) on Tuesday said a subsidiary of the company has signed a definitive agreement to sell substantially all of the assets of 180-bed Crestwood Medical Center in Huntsville, Alabama, and its associated outpatient centers and practices, to Huntsville Hospital Health System for $450 million.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter of 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amalgamated Bank raises dividend by 21%
data.description:
• Amalgamated Bank (AMAL) declared $0.17/share quarterly dividend, 21.4% increase from prior dividend of $0.14.
  • Forward yield 2.01%
  • Payable Feb. 19; for shareholders of record Feb. 3; ex-div Feb. 3.
  • See AMAL Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Coca-Cola and Procter & Gamble outperform on a bruising day overall for U.S. stocks
data.description:
Coca-Cola Company (KO) and Procter & Gamble (PG) outperformed on Tuesday amid a broad market decline tied to President Donald Trump's threat to impose new tariffs on European nations opposing the sale of Greenland to the United States. U.S. Treasury yields spiked, and the U.S. dollar fell as part of a flight to quality that also drew investors into defensive favorites Coca-Cola (KO) and Procter & Gamble (PG).

Coca-Cola (KO) closed with a 1.9% gain, while Procter & Gamble (PG) pushed 1.7% higher as the two consumer staples stocks stood on a day that 27 of the 30 Dow Jones Industrial Average constituents were down for the session. Procter & Gamble (PG) is due to report earnings on Thursday, while Coca-Cola (KO) steps into the earnings confessional during the second week of February.

Looking ahead, there is not expected to be a dramatic improvement in U.S.-European trade relations during the World Economic Forum in Davos this week, although analysts have pointed to the one-year track record of tariff threats being reeled in by President Trump within weeks and months of the initial threats as negotiations spin on.

data.title: SurgePays launches underwritten public offering of shares and warrants
data.description:
• SurgePays (SURG) said on Tuesday it has commenced an underwritten public offering of its common stock and/or pre-funded warrants.
  • The company said all securities in the offering will be sold by SurgePays and that the size and terms of the offering will depend on market conditions. 
  • SurgePays said the offering is being made pursuant to an existing shelf registration statement on file with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • SURG -10.75% after hours to $1.66.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Netflix GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01, revenue of $12.05B beats by $80M
data.description:
• Netflix (NFLX): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.56 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $12.05B (+17.6% Y/Y) beats by $80M.
  • 1Q26 Revenue of $12.16B vs $12.17B consensus, EPS of $0.76 vs $0.81 consensus
  • For 2026, based on F/X rates as of 1/1/2026, we forecast revenue of $50.7B-$51.7B vs $50.98B consensus. This represents12%-14% year over year growth (or 11%-13% F/X neutral growth), driven by increases in membershipand pricing plus a projected rough doubling of ad revenue in 2026 vs. 2025.We’re targeting a 2026 operating margin of 31.5% (based on 1/1/26 F/X rates), up from 29.5% in 2025,which includes approximately $275M of acquisition-related expenses.
  • Press Release

data.title: Edison cut on Eaton Fire overhang at Wells Fargo; Public Service upgraded
data.description:
Edison International (EIX) -3.9% in Tuesday's trading as Wells Fargo downgraded the utility to Underweight from Equal Weight with a $59 price target, citing the Eaton Fire liability overhang, uncertainty around the California liability construct, and the state's regulatory environment moving forward.

Wells Fargo's Shahriar Pourreza said a full picture of the Eaton Fire investigation remains unclear, and liability estimates remain an overhang for Edison (EIX) in and of itself, and the analyst said he is awaiting more information on official cause and incremental news on the scale of claims to be paid as they get tallied up; the recent announcement of a lawsuit against Los Angeles County and several water agencies as cross plaintiffs is "a potential positive, but introduces complexity and longer timeline."

The other key outstanding piece of the Edison (EIX) story is the larger California wildfire liability
landscape, Pourreza wrote; while the company has mostly wrapped up its regulatory activity including the general rate case, legacy fire claims and cost of capital, the analyst still sees California as a high-risk environment entering a gubernatorial election year without an incumbent governor and as state wildfire liability remains an open question.

At the same time, Pourreza and his utilities team upgraded Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $92 PT, pointing to "a solid management team and fundamentals converging with attractive valuation and enhanced margin opportunity above the PTC levels in guidance."

Wells Fargo also downgraded Southern Co. (SO), OGE Energy (OGE), NorthWestern (NWE) and Idacorp (IDA) to Underperform from Equal Weight, all on valuation.
